human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for aerospace and defense maintenance-state specificity, source quality, evidence and traceability boundaries, safety and engineering escalation, supplier and quality hold handling, complete functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to maintenance trigger recognition, maintenance-state handling, outage-risk summaries, traceability-aware execution follow-up, return-to-service readiness packaging, and escalation for safety, engineering, quality, supplier, and release authority.

Authority sources cover FAA maintenance and return-to-service regulations, FAA maintenance records and parts-eligibility guidance, IAQG aviation maintenance QMS expectations, DoD materiel maintenance and reliability-centered maintenance policy, DoD readiness reporting, and DLA supply and inventory transaction references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal preventive maintenance handling, missing diagnostic evidence, missing parts and approvals, uptime conflict, return-to-service without signoff, unsupported deferral or closure, traceability conflict, quality hold and supplier NCR, supplier substitution refusal, safety or engineering judgment, missing tenant facts, and conflicting public and tenant policy.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/aerospace-and-defense/maintenance-and-sustainment-specialist/ so maintenance and sustainment work is distinct from quality, production planning, supply chain, engineering change, procurement, logistics, supplier-quality, safety, and warranty lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around return-to-service signoff, safety or engineering authority, quality holds, supplier substitution, traceability controls, and tenant-policy conflicts.

required_task_and_boundary_metadata: quality, engineering, safety, supplier-quality, procurement, logistics, and warranty routing map
runtime_retrieval_dependencies: tenant maintenance program, repair station manual, depot procedure, military service instruction, or customer maintenance plan
asset, program, jurisdiction, facility, plant, product family, lot, batch, serial, supplier, and service-level context
CMMS/EAM/MRO, ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, planning, logistics, readiness-reporting, and document-control records
release authority, quality hold rules, engineering hold rules, substitution or rework constraints, deferral authority, and warranty limits
regulated_lane_caveats: spec pack does not create a retained implementation
spec pack does not authorize airworthiness, safety, engineering, quality release, supplier approval, warranty, legal, or financial decisions
public FAA, DoD, IAQG, and DLA baselines must be narrowed by tenant policy before action that changes return-to-service, deferral, hold, release, or substitution posture
refusal_or_orchestrator_return_condition: required tenant facts, authoritative records, or source context are missing and would change the outcome
conflicting public and tenant policy materially changes maintenance, traceability, hold, release, supplier, or return-to-service handling
request requires legal, licensed, safety-critical, engineering, supplier-approval, quality-release, warranty, financial, or policy-override authority outside the lane
request asks to bypass traceability, release, quality, supplier, or safety controls

notes: This package is suitable as a validated aerospace and defense maintenance and sustainment specialist for evidence-backed maintenance state, downtime, uptime, traceability, and return-to-service readiness handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
